title: Hieracium reclinatiforme
taxonomy: Hieracium reclinatiforme is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as hawkweeds. Hieracium reclinatiforme is further grouped into the subgenus Pilosella, which is a subset of the genus Hieracium. The subgenus Pilosella is characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. Within the subgenus Pilosella, Hieracium reclinatiforme is classified as a species of hawkweed.

intro: Hieracium reclinatiforme, commonly known as spotted hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium reclinatiforme include flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Hieracium reclinatiforme is often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression.
